page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 32 physical cores mapped to 32 vCPUs under a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10 percent, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression is average. However, multi-threaded operations show stronger results, with multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 25 percent and Passmark CPU encryption testing also reaching the top 25 percent. General multi-core metrics, including Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, fall within the middle 50 percent average range. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput also perform at average levels.

The shared CPU allocation and lower single-core performance represent notable tradeoffs, making this server less suitable for single-threaded tasks or latency-sensitive real-time applications. However, the high core density relative to memory capacity offers qualitative cost efficiency for parallelizable workloads that do not require dedicated CPU pinning. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded data decompression, batch processing, static web serving, and parallelized software compilation.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 server featuring 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model under KVM virtualization, this server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a significant divergence: single-core CPU performance is in the bottom 10 percent, whereas multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and Passmark encryption, rank in the top 25 percent. Most other metrics, including memory bandwidth and static web serving, perform in the average middle 50 percent range. Given the 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio and shared CPU resources, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, non-latency-sensitive workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM. It features strong multi-threaded decompression and encryption performance alongside shared CPU allocation.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-32xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and shared CPU allocation, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A multi-core x86_64 compute platform featuring thirty-two shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
64.0 GB of system memory with 2.0 GB per core
Strong multi-threaded decompression and encryption performance
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for parallel batch processing and software compilation
